creator: Amanda Silberling
title: (No AI) Hack suggests AI music generator Suno scraped YouTube for training data
dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
content: The hacker used an employee's credentials to access source code, which revealed how Suno scraped decades of audio.
contentSnippet: The hacker used an employee's credentials to access source code, which revealed how Suno scraped decades of audio.

creator: Lauren Forristal
title: (No AI) Whatnot acquires Shaped to power real-time live shopping recommendations
dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
content: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot has acquired AI startup Shaped, a machine learning company focused on real-time recommendations and search. The deal will bolster Whatnot’s personalization and discovery features as it expands into new product categories.
contentSnippet: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot has acquired AI startup Shaped, a machine learning company focused on real-time recommendations and search. The deal will bolster Whatnot’s personalization and discovery features as it expands into new product categories.

title: (No AI) OpenAI finally launches hardware… for Codex
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Supply-Co-Page_Brand.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI is finally releasing some hardware. No, it isn't the mysterious AI-powered device the company is [developing with former Apple designer Jony Ive](https://www.theverge.com/news/827607/openai-hardware-prototype-chatgpt-jony-ive-sam-altman), a project already [tangled up in a messy lawsuit](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/965294/openai-apple-trade-secrets-lawsuit-sam-altman-ipo). Instead, it's a product designed to be used with its coding platform, Codex.

The device, a square-shaped block of buttons called Codex Micro, is a collaboration between the AI company and keyboard maker Work Louder. OpenAI said it is a limited-run collaboration that will give users more ways to monitor and manage their agents.

The pad closely resembles Work Louder's [Creator Micro 2](https://worklouder.cc/creator-micro-2), and marketing images show what appears to be an identical …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/965901/openai-hardware-codex-micro-launch)

OpenAI is finally releasing some hardware. No, it isn't the mysterious AI-powered device the company is developing with former Apple designer Jony Ive, a project already tangled up in a messy lawsuit. Instead, it's a product designed to be used with its coding platform, Codex. 
The device, a square-shaped block of buttons called Codex Micro, is a collaboration between the AI company and keyboard maker Work Louder. OpenAI said it is a limited-run collaboration that will give users more ways to monitor and manage their agents. 
The pad closely resembles Work Louder's Creator Micro 2, and marketing images show what appears to be an identical  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) OpenAI may announce a ChatGPT smart speaker this year
author: Emma Roth
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STK155_OPEN_AI_4_CVirginia_D.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI's first device is set to be a smart speaker that lets you talk with ChatGPT, according to a report [from _Bloomberg_](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-14/openai-s-first-device-will-be-moveable-screenless-speaker-built-as-ai-companion?accessToken=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzb3VyY2UiOiJTdWJzY3JpYmVyR2lmdGVkQXJ0aWNsZSIsImlhdCI6MTc4NDA2MjAxMywiZXhwIjoxNzg0NjY2ODEzLCJhcnRpY2xlSWQiOiJUSTYwSllUOU5KTFMwMCIsImJjb25uZWN0SWQiOiJDNEVEQ0FFMUZBMDU0MEJFQTI0QTlGMjExQzFFOTA4MCJ9.DfRN0afk0TFIaHFw9zEKYjehnfMsZfKC7gPoVos8WPI&leadSource=uverify%20wall). The device apparently won't have a screen, but will use a camera and additional sensors to "understand" your environment.

The report comes just days after Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI that accused the AI company of [stealing hardware secrets](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964843/apple-openai-lawsuit-wildest-claims). OpenAI, in a [new statement on Tuesday](https://www.theverge.com/tech/965649/openai-has-a-new-statement-about-apples-lawsuit), said that it is "not aware of any evidence that this complaint has merit."

Sources tell _Bloomberg_ that OpenAI's device will also feature a rechargeable battery that will allow users to carry it with them. It will offer smart home contro …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/965670/openai-chatgpt-ai-smart-speaker-hardware-device)

The report comes just days after Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI that accused the AI company of stealing hardware secrets. OpenAI, in a new statement on Tuesday, said that it is "not aware of any evidence that this complaint has merit."
Sources tell Bloomberg that OpenAI's device will also feature a rechargeable battery that will allow users to carry it with them. It will offer smart home contro …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The report comes just days after Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI that accused […]

title: (No AI) The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
content: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.
contentSnippet: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

title: (No AI) GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
content: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.
contentSnippet: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.

title: (No AI) Empowering India’s next generation of innovators with ATL Saathi
content: Google and AIM launched ATL Saathi, a Gemini-powered AI tool empowering Indian educators in robotics labs.

creator: Isabella Ward
title: (No AI) An Inventor of Apple’s FaceID Wants to Analyze Your Brain’s Health With AI
dc:creator: Isabella Ward
content: Gidi Littwin’s new AI startup, Hemispheric, makes diagnostic brain scans for conditions like depression, PTSD, and Parkinson’s. He wants the technology to be as cheap and easy as for a blood test.
contentSnippet: Gidi Littwin’s new AI startup, Hemispheric, makes diagnostic brain scans for conditions like depression, PTSD, and Parkinson’s. He wants the technology to be as cheap and easy as for a blood test.

creator: Dillon Thompson
title: (No AI) My Ebike Delivery Went Missing. When I Tried to Recover It, I Ended Up in Chatbot Hell
dc:creator: Dillon Thompson
content: Companies’ increasing reliance on AI chatbots isn’t making the customer service experience smarter. It’s just making it more infuriating.
contentSnippet: Companies’ increasing reliance on AI chatbots isn’t making the customer service experience smarter. It’s just making it more infuriating.
